text: The greatest improvements in VO2max occur at exercise intensities of 90-100% of VO2max, regardless of duration, frequency, programme length, or initial fitness level.
When exercise duration exceeds 35 minutes, lower-intensity training produces similar VO2max improvements as higher-intensity training of shorter duration.
Exercise frequency as low as 2 times per week can improve VO2max in less fit individuals.
When aerobic power exceeds 50 ml/kg/min, a minimum exercise frequency of 3 times per week is required to improve VO2max.
As initial fitness level increases, the magnitude of VO2max improvement decreases regardless of training intensity, frequency, or duration.
Maximal gains in aerobic power are elicited with training at 90-100% VO2max, 4 times per week, for 35-45 minutes per session.
Lower training intensities still produce effective VO2max improvements and reduce injury risk in non-athletic populations.
